violations.description: JSON-RPC Error Code Violation: Parse Error
violations.recommendation: This specific error code test failed. Ensure the server returns standard JSON-RPC error codes for known error conditions: -32700 (parse error), -32600 (invalid request), -32601 (method not found), -32602 (invalid params), -32603 (internal error). See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc#errors

violations.description: JSON-RPC Error Code Violation: Invalid Request - Missing jsonrpc
violations.recommendation: This specific error code test failed. Ensure the server returns standard JSON-RPC error codes for known error conditions: -32700 (parse error), -32600 (invalid request), -32601 (method not found), -32602 (invalid params), -32603 (internal error). See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc#errors

violations.description: JSON-RPC Error Code Violation: Method Not Found
violations.recommendation: This specific error code test failed. Ensure the server returns standard JSON-RPC error codes for known error conditions: -32700 (parse error), -32600 (invalid request), -32601 (method not found), -32602 (invalid params), -32603 (internal error). See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc#errors

violations.description: JSON-RPC Error Code Violation: Invalid Params
violations.recommendation: This specific error code test failed. Ensure the server returns standard JSON-RPC error codes for known error conditions: -32700 (parse error), -32600 (invalid request), -32601 (method not found), -32602 (invalid params), -32603 (internal error). See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc#errors

violations.description: Request format does not comply with JSON-RPC 2.0 specification
violations.recommendation: Ensure every JSON-RPC request includes "jsonrpc": "2.0", a string "method" field, and an integer "id". The "params" field, if present, must be an object or array. See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc

violations.description: Response format does not comply with JSON-RPC 2.0 specification
violations.recommendation: Every JSON-RPC response must include "jsonrpc": "2.0" and the same "id" as the request. Successful responses use a "result" field; failures use an "error" object with code and message. See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc

violations.description: Batch processing implementation is inconsistent or incomplete
violations.recommendation: If the server accepts batch requests, it must process each request independently and return an array of responses in any order. Ensure batch handling does not drop or duplicate responses. See https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#batch

violations.description: Error codes do not comply with JSON-RPC 2.0 standard error codes
violations.recommendation: Review all error responses to ensure they use standard JSON-RPC 2.0 error codes. Custom error codes must be outside the reserved range (-32000 to -32099 for server errors). See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc#errors

protocolCompliance.message: roots/list: supported | logging/setLevel: supported | sampling/createMessage: supported | completion/complete: supported

findings.summary: Server responded to roots/list, indicating optional roots workflow support.
findings.recommendation: Keep capability advertisement and method support aligned so clients can rely on initialize without speculative probing.

findings.summary: Server responded to logging/setLevel, indicating optional logging controls are available.
findings.recommendation: Keep capability advertisement and method support aligned so clients can rely on initialize without speculative probing.

findings.summary: Server responded to sampling/createMessage, indicating optional sampling workflows are available.
findings.recommendation: Keep capability advertisement and method support aligned so clients can rely on initialize without speculative probing.

findings.summary: Server responded to completion/complete, indicating optional completions are available.
findings.recommendation: Keep capability advertisement and method support aligned so clients can rely on initialize without speculative probing.

findings.summary: Server responds to logging/setLevel but does not advertise the logging capability during initialize.
findings.recommendation: Declare logging in initialize when logging/setLevel is supported.

findings.summary: Server responds to completion/complete but does not advertise the completions capability during initialize.
findings.recommendation: Declare completions in initialize when completion/complete is supported.

findings.summary: Notification handling probe inconclusive due to transient transport pressure (HTTP 429).
findings.recommendation: Rerun protocol validation against a lower-pressure window before treating notification handling as non-compliant.

recommendations: Spec: This specific error code test failed. Ensure the server returns standard JSON-RPC error codes for known error conditions: -32700 (parse error), -32600 (invalid request), -32601 (method not found), -32602 (invalid params), -32603 (internal error). See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc#errors

Spec: Ensure every JSON-RPC request includes "jsonrpc": "2.0", a string "method" field, and an integer "id". The "params" field, if present, must be an object or array. See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc

Spec: Every JSON-RPC response must include "jsonrpc": "2.0" and the same "id" as the request. Successful responses use a "result" field; failures use an "error" object with code and message. See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc

Spec: Review all error responses to ensure they use standard JSON-RPC 2.0 error codes. Custom error codes must be outside the reserved range (-32000 to -32099 for server errors). See https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/json-rpc#errors

Spec: If the server accepts batch requests, it must process each request independently and return an array of responses in any order. Ensure batch handling does not drop or duplicate responses. See https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#batch

Operational: Investigate why the performance probe ended without captured measurements (Public remote synthetic load probe did not capture measurements before timeout/cancellation; results are reported as advisory and excluded from pass/fail decisions.) before treating runtime behavior as representative
